title: Enigma Stage 3 Postmortem — Deep Code-Level Optimizer Mutations
objective: Generate code-level mutations via deep hypothesis generation with set-level gap analysis, compound top winners, validate at 5000-step horizon on real GPT-2 training

stage2_recap.key_lesson: All Stage 2 code-level mutations (bf16 polar, simplified second moment, reordered trust, trust-gated WD) failed at 1000-step horizon despite some winning at 200 steps

methodology.hypothesis_generation: Deep Enigma doctrine with 3 parallel subagents covering trust variants, pipeline structure, and production parity gaps
methodology.set_level_gap_analysis: Deduplication, coverage map across 5 pipeline stages (momentum, ortho, second_moment, trust, WD), blind spot identification, portfolio selection
methodology.portfolio_design: 8 hypotheses across 5 pipeline stages (3 exploit, 3 scout, 1 wildcard, 1 dropped)
evaluation_phases: Phase 1: Toy screening (24 steps) — validity gate only
Phase 2: GPT-2 1000-step evaluation — all 8 mutations vs H02 baseline
Phase 3: 5000-step compound evaluation — top 3 singles + 2 compounds

portfolio.title: Soft cautious WD with sigmoid alignment
Cautious WD on raw gradient alignment
Asymmetric trust — soft floor, hard ceiling

phase2_results_1000steps.improvement_vs_stage2: Stage 2 had 0/4 code-level winners at 1000 steps; Stage 3 has 7/8

phase3_results_5000steps.key_finding: H41 solo is the clear winner — its advantage DOUBLED from 1k (+0.015) to 5k (+0.032). Compounding hurts.

ranking_evolution.description: How rankings shifted across horizons — the meta-lesson continues

H41_momentum_warmup.trajectory: Strengthens with horizon. Only mutation that consistently improves.

H39_perneuron_trust.trajectory: Stable but doesn't grow. Reliable second-tier.

H46_nesterov_decouple.5000_steps: Not tested solo; compound H41+H46 LOSES (-0.010)
H46_nesterov_decouple.trajectory: REVERSAL when combined with H41. Interferes with momentum warmup.

COMPOUND_H41_H39_H46.trajectory: Worse than H41 alone (0.017 vs 0.032). H39+H46 drag it down.

COMPOUND_H41_H46.trajectory: CATASTROPHIC. H46 cancels H41's warmup benefit. Decoupled Nesterov + warmup = bad interaction.

negative_knowledge.NK12: H44 (post-ortho scale preservation): -0.066 BPB at 1000 steps. Polar Express norm distortion is beneficial implicit regularization — DO NOT preserve pre-ortho scale.
negative_knowledge.NK13: Compounding top mutations does NOT automatically help. H41+H39+H46 compound (Δ=+0.017) is worse than H41 alone (Δ=+0.032). The compound cuts the gain in half.
negative_knowledge.NK14: H46 (decoupled Nesterov blend 0.8) actively interferes with H41 (momentum warmup). H41+H46 without H39 LOSES (-0.010). These touch the same method and create conflicting dynamics.
negative_knowledge.NK15: H59 (soft cautious WD): marginal +0.001 at 1000 steps. Not worth the complexity.
negative_knowledge.NK16: H36 (asymmetric trust): marginal +0.002 at 1000 steps. Sigmoid floor adds complexity for negligible gain.

positive_knowledge.PK01: H41 (momentum warmup 0.85→0.95 over 200 steps): +0.032 BPB at 5000 steps. The single best mutation across all stages. Effect GROWS with training length.
positive_knowledge.PK02: H39 (per-neuron trust, aspect-aware): +0.014 BPB, stable across 1k and 5k horizons. Reliable but doesn't compound well with momentum changes.
positive_knowledge.PK03: H42 (second moment bias correction for first 50 steps): +0.007 BPB at 1000 steps. Small but principled — untested at 5k.
positive_knowledge.PK04: H54 (raw gradient alignment for cautious WD): +0.007 BPB at 1000 steps. Principled — untested at 5k.

meta_lessons.lesson: Deep hypothesis generation with set-level gap analysis dramatically outperforms random exploration
meta_lessons.evidence: Stage 2 code-level: 0/4 winners at 1000 steps. Stage 3: 7/8 winners. The shared context file + 3 parallel subagents + coverage-based portfolio selection was the key methodological improvement.

meta_lessons.lesson: Compounding mutations is NOT free — interactions can cancel benefits
meta_lessons.evidence: H41 solo (+0.032) > H41+H39+H46 compound (+0.017) > H41+H46 (-0.010). More mutations ≠ more gain.

meta_lessons.lesson: Mutations that strengthen with horizon length are the most valuable
meta_lessons.evidence: H41 went from +0.015 at 1k to +0.032 at 5k — it doubled. H39 stayed flat at +0.014. H41 is fundamentally better.

meta_lessons.lesson: Momentum is the highest-leverage part of the optimizer pipeline
meta_lessons.evidence: H41 (momentum warmup) is the best single mutation. H46 (Nesterov blend) was #3 at 1k. Both modify _apply_momentum. The momentum schedule has more room for improvement than trust, WD, or second moment.

meta_lessons.lesson: Mutations to the same method interact unpredictably
meta_lessons.evidence: H41 and H46 both modify _apply_momentum. Each wins alone at 1k steps, but H41+H46 LOSES at 5k. The warmup schedule and the fixed Nesterov blend create conflicting early-training dynamics.

meta_lessons.lesson: Single clean mutations beat compound stacks
meta_lessons.evidence: Across all stages: H02 alone > H02+clip+gate (Stage 2 result). H41 alone > H41+H39+H46 (Stage 3 result). Simplicity wins.

meta_lessons.lesson: The 5x horizon test is essential — 1000-step rankings are not final
meta_lessons.evidence: H41's advantage doubled at 5k. H46 looked good at 1k but kills H41 at 5k. Rankings are unstable below 5k steps.

final_recommendation.best_optimizer: H02 (layerwise trust ratio [0.5, 1.5]) + H41 (momentum warmup 0.85→0.95 over 200 steps)
final_recommendation.improvement_vs_production_muon: +0.032 BPB vs H02 baseline, which itself was +0.006 vs production Muon at 1000 steps
final_recommendation.code_change: Override _apply_momentum: linearly warm beta from 0.85 to spec.momentum (0.95) over first 200 steps. 4 lines of code.
next_steps: Test H41 at 10k+ steps to confirm continued strengthening
Test H41 + H42 (bias correction) — independent methods, likely safe to compound
Test H41 + H54 (raw grad WD) — independent methods, likely safe to compound
Do NOT compound H41 with H46 or any other momentum mutation
Consider deploying H41 to production Muon — the warmup schedule is trivially portable
